editAgentRounds.reply:
You typed "ntiue" — I don't understand that. I'll propose a short plan and some likely actions; tell me which one you want or correct the typo and I'll proceed.

Planned checklist (pick one or tell me another):
- [ ] Clarify what "ntiue" means (typo / command / continue run / fix resources)
- [ ] If you meant "continue": investigate missing resource error (ImageIO.read returned null) and search for `/res/` assets in the repo
- [ ] Optionally: attempt to fix by ensuring resource files are on the classpath and re-run the app

Current status (what I already did):
- I ran `main.Main` successfully using Java 25. It failed at runtime with:
  - Exception: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: input == null!
  - Origin: `Resource.getImage(...)` (loads from `RES_IMAGE_PATH = "/res/image/"`)
  - This means a requested image resource wasn't found on the classpath (likely missing or in the wrong folder).

Helpful next actions (choose one):
1) "Continue" — I'll automatically search the workspace for the `res` directories and list any images (I'll then put them on the classpath or show how to run again).
2) "Fix and run" — I'll try to make the assets available on the runtime classpath (copy or configure) and re-run the app until it starts cleanly (I can do this autonomously).
3) "Show the error" — I'll paste the exact stack trace and the file/line referencing the missing resource (already partially shown).
4) Something else — tell me what you meant.

Which would you like me to do?

turns.suggestedTitle: Resolving Missing Image Resources in Java Project

editAgentRounds.reply: I'll locate the exact resource being requested (causing ImageIO.read to get null) by checking `main.Main` and searching the project for references to Resource.getImage or RES_IMAGE_PATH; then I'll list the `assets/res` and `src/res` directories to see if the image files exist. I'll run the app again after fixing the classpath if needed. Expected outcome: identify missing resource and make the runtime find it (or explain how to run correctly).

I'll list the project's assets and source resource directories to find where image files are stored (search for dq1.png or res/image). This will tell us why Resource.getImage returned null and how to fix it.

I will inspect how resources are packaged into the build/classpath. The code uses Class.getResourceAsStream with paths like "/res/image/dq1.png", so assets under `assets/res` need to be on the runtime classpath (often by copying into `build/classes/res`). Check build output `build/classes` for a `res` folder; earlier listing showed no `res`. I'll search for Ant build rules or copy tasks in build scripts; but simpler: copy the `assets/res` directory into `build/classes/res` so Resource.class.getResourceAsStream can find them. I'll copy the assets into `build/classes/res` by creating the directory and copying files. I'll run the java again using Java 25.
